title: dahlia cervantesii
intro: Dahlia cervantesii, also known as the Mexican dahlia or Dahlia coccinea, is a type of flowering plant in the dahlia family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Dahlia cervantesii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from digestive issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to alleviate digestive issues.
